hypothesis: Generating shell-wise compositions under fixed cutoff radii (2.5 Å and 5.0 Å) will produce catalytically suboptimal surface designs for adsorbates with significantly different van der Waals radii or binding geometries, and incorporating adsorbate-dependent dynamic shell definitions into the conditional diffusion model will improve predicted adsorption energy accuracy by at least 15% compared to fixed-radius shells.
